text.heading: Tension at town hall meeting as CDA chairman, accuses LCDA Chairman
text.p: The town hall meeting held at Alimosho for residents would have degenerated into confusion if not for the prompt intervention of the Speaker, Rt. Hon Adeyemi Ikuforiji. Tempers rose when Mrs Janet Akinyemi, Chairman, CDA Shagari Estate accused Mosan/Okunola LCDA Chairman, Hon. Rasheed Abiodun Mafe of not carrying CDA's and CDC's in the area along. This was countered by a serious protest by supporters of Mafe.

Her words: ‚ÄúOur own chairman is the problem we have in Mosan-Okunola, because he has not been carrying us along in all he has been doing. He has not done anything tangible in the area and ever since he became the chairman, no development had taken place.

‚ÄúOur LCDA ought to have developed like other local governments and local councils in the state, but because our chairman has neglected the people in the council, no development has taken place, he has been running a one-man show government‚Ä?.

While Adeyemi was still speaking, a group of women in the uniformed attire known as aso ebi rushed out to attack her claiming that all she was saying was nothing but lies. They claimed that she was sponsored by a member of the House of Assembly, Onibiyo Adelabu representing Alimosho Constituency I, to come and tarnish the Chairman‚Äôs image.
It took the intervention of the Speaker to bring the situation under control, as Ikuforiji had to leave his seat on the high table to prevail on the women who had almost manhandled Adeyemi.

Reacting, Mosan-Okunola Local Council Development Area, Chairman, Abiodun Mafe expressed displeasure at Adeyemi‚Äôs utterances. He explained that all what she uttered while she was speaking was nothing but fallacy.

According to him, ‚Äúwhen I assumed office as the chairman, I called all the CDAs and CDCs Chairmen and we had a meeting and they made me understood all those things they needed and I have started working on some of those requests. I don‚Äôt know why somebody would just come out here and be saying what is not true.

‚ÄúDuring the primaries, somebody contested from her side and because the person lost, they have been an opposition to most of the things we have been doing. You as journalists can go around and conduct your investigations; then you will see most of the things we have done,‚Ä? Mafe stated.

Other requests by residents are that the government should work on the Ayobo- Ipaja road that links Sango- Ota in Ogun state, other roads they want the government to work on are Akesan-Obadore road, Isiba- Agodo road, and that Pako to Isheri road should be extended to join Jakande road. They also want drainage to be constructed on Ikotun- Ijegun road.
Residents also want extension of canal from Agege/Dopemu to Command. Other request include Bore hole, security, transformers, potable drinking water etc.
In his address earlier, speaker of the House, Adeyemi Ikuforiji assured the residents that the House would weigh their feelings and mirror their needs to bring about a better society.
‚ÄúCreating linkages between the government and the governed is a major focus of this democratic- strengthening program embarked upon by the Lagos dtate House of Assembly‚Ä?, Ikuforiji said.

Also in his welcome address earlier Hon. Onibiyo Adelabu, representing Alimosho 1 in the House told his constituents that the town hall meeting is a major decentralized program that takes lawmaking to the people.

‚ÄúAnd as elected officials we are responsible for development planning and pilot projects ‚Ä¶this is a golden opportunity that must not elude you as every community ought to have social amenities and your request through this forum would be channeled to the appropriate authorities‚Ä?.
